summary: Cholinergic drugs like pilocarpine and cevimeline stimulate saliva production via activation of muscarinic receptors, whereas anticholinergic drugs such as atropine reduce saliva output due to blocking these same receptors; thus, cholinergic medications effectively increase saliva levels when needed. Adrenergic drug classes have varying indirect influences but generally do not significantly impact salivation under normal circumstances without specific therapeutic interventions targeting their direct action pathways involved primarily within parasympathetic nervous system regulation over exocrine functions including those governing oral secretions necessary maintaining proper hydration mucosal integrity preventing infections among other critical roles ensuring optimal health functioning well-being overall systemic homeostasis balance stability resilience adaptability flexibility
